questionText: The magnetic dipoles in a magnetic domain of a ferromagnetic material interact strongly, resulting in spontaneous alignment of the magnetic dipoles in the domain.

subject: Physics
questionText: An unmagnetized ferromagnetic material contains magnetic domains in which the magnetic dipoles are randomly oriented.

subject: Physics
questionText: An echo is a manifestation of reflection of sound waves.

subject: Physics
questionText: Diffraction of sound waves makes it possible to hear around corners.

subject: Physics
questionText: Because sound waves are longitudinal waves, interference of sound waves is impossible.

subject: Physics
questionText: An ideal gas effuses at a rate that is independent of the pressure and temperature of the gas.

subject: Physics
questionText: The rate of effusion of an ideal gas is directly proportional to the temperature of the gas.

subject: Physics
questionText: The rate of effusion of an ideal gas is directly proportional to the square root of the temperature of the gas.

subject: Physics
questionText: The wavelength of the fundamental note of a pipe closed at one end is four times the length of the pipe if end effects are neglected.

subject: Physics
questionText: The wavelength of the first overtone of a pipe closed at one end is two times the length of the pipe if end effects are neglected.

subject: Physics
questionText: The frequency of the first overtone of a pipe closed at one end is three times the frequency of the fundamental note.

subject: Physics
questionText: The divergence of the leaf of a positively charged gold leaf electroscope decreases when a positively charged rod is brought near the cap of the electroscope.

subject: Physics
questionText: The divergence of the leaf of a negatively charged gold leaf electroscope increases when a negatively charged rod is brought near the cap of the electroscope.

questionText: The divergence of the leaf of a negatively charged gold leaf electroscope increases when a positively charged rod is brought near the cap of the electroscope.

subject: Physics
questionText: An n type semiconductor is obtained by introducing electron donors into a pure semiconductor, thereby creating a negatively charged material.

subject: Physics
questionText: An intrinsic semiconductor contains as many electrons in the conduction band as holes in the valence band.

subject: Physics
questionText: A p type semiconductor contains more electrons in the conduction band than holes in the valence band.

subject: Physics
questionText: An electron moving in a uniform magnetic field experiences a magnetic force that is perpendicular to the magnetic field.

subject: Physics
questionText: An electron moving in a uniform magnetic field experiences a magnetic force that is perpendicular to the velocity of the electron.

subject: Physics
questionText: An electron moving in a uniform magnetic field undergoes a change in kinetic energy that is proportional to the magnetic field strength.

subject: Physics
questionText: The alternating current through a resistor is in phase with the alternating voltage across it.

subject: Physics
questionText: The average power dissipated in a resistor when an alternating current flows through it equals half the peak current times the peak voltage.

subject: Physics
questionText: The average power dissipated in a resistor when an alternating current flows through it equals half the peak current times the rms voltage.

subject: Physics
questionText: The proportional limit of a material is the maximum stress that can be applied without permanent deformation.

subject: Physics
questionText: The elastic limit of a material is the maximum stress that is still proportional to strain.

subject: Physics
questionText: Young’s modulus of a material is the ratio of stress to strain for stresses lower than the proportional limit.

subject: Physics
questionText: The image formed o the screen of a pinhole camera is always upright.

subject: Physics
questionText: The height of the image in a pinhole camera is always equals the height of the screen.

subject: Physics
questionText: If an object is closer to the hole of a pinhole camera than the screen, the image of the object is larger than the object.

subject: Physics
questionText: The separation between adjacent energy levels of the hydrogen atom decreases as the principal quantum number decreases.

subject: Physics
questionText: The energy difference between the ground state and the first excited state of the hydrogen atom is largest for any pair of adjacent levels of the hydrogen atom.

subject: Physics
questionText: The longest transition wavelength in the hydrogen spectrum is associated with the ground and first excited states.

subject: Physics
questionText: The energy stored by an inductor varies in direct proportion to the current through it.

questionText: The energy stored by an inductor depends on the inductance of the inductor.

subject: Physics
questionText: When the current through an inductor is doubled, the energy stored in the inductor is halved.

questionText: The most intense wavelength in a black body spectrum is inversely proportional to the temperature of the blackbody.

questionText: The Rayleigh-Jeans law correctly describes the short wavelength portion of the radiation emitted by a black body.

subject: Physics

questionText: The mechanical advantage of a simple machine is the ratio of the magnitude of the effort to that of the load.

subject: Physics
questionText: The efficiency of a simple machine is the ratio of work output to work input.

subject: Physics

subject: Physics
questionText: Lenz’s law implies that if the flux linkage in a region is increasing, the induced electromotive force is such as to decrease the flux linkage.

subject: Physics
questionText: The resultant of two forces that make an angle of 30° to each other has a magnitude less than the magnitude of any of the forces.

questionText: The resultant of two perpendicular forces has a magnitude that is less than the magnitude of the sum of the magnitudes of the forces.

subject: Physics
questionText: Power is the rate of doing work.
